violations.text: The door to the outside, located in the back of kitchen, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. PIC showed inspector new door sweep. Maintenace pending to install it. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025

Observed employee washing, rinsing and sanitizing at 3 bay sink without beard restraint and handling food. FOOD EMPLOYEES shall wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings or nets, beard restraints, and clothing that covers body hair, that are designed and worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed FOOD; clean EQUIPMENT, UTENSILS, and LINENS; and unwrapped SINGLE SERVICE and SINGLE-USE ARTICLES. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the sandwich prep unit flip top and adjacent wall haves an accumulation of food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The ceiling in the prep area has an accumulation of dust. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 02/19/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the speed racks have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024

Rice and mac and cheese was observed held at 118-120 degrees Fahrenheit in the 2-door hot holding unit. The ambient temperature of the hot-holding unit was observed at 113 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, foods were stored in the unit over 3.5 hours prior to inspection. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of item. A chicken patty was observed to be 120 in the hot-holding unit above the refrigeration prep unit. PIC elected to voluntarily dispose of item at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024

The 2-door hot-holding unit was observed to be in poor repair. The unit was observed to be set to 175 degrees Fahrenheit and was maintaining an ambient temperature of 113 degrees Fahrenheit. The unit is to not be used until it can be replaced/repaired/serviced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the fan covers in the walk-in cooler and the speed racks have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/03/2024

Chicken nuggets were observed held at 123 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was recently cooked. PIC elected disposal. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

One staff member was observed not wearing a beard restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Beard guard was used by employee at the time of inspection. Proper hair restraints reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023

The prep unit holding shelf stable mayo was observed holding an ambient temperature of 46 degrees. Mayo in unit was 46 degrees as well. PIC stated unit should be holding at 41 degrees or less. The hot hold unit for chicken nuggets was observed not holding the food at 135 or above. Unit taken out of service and shall not be used until repaired/replaced/adjusted. New Violation. Correct By: 04/26/2023
